headlines.title: Euro slips as soft Eurozone inflation, Japan pension shift lift Yen
Japan's finance minister urges pension funds to boost domestic investments, sending yen higher
Takaichi's pension pivot seeks to reverse Abe-era outpouring of Japanese capital
Japan Moves to Boost Sickly Yen With Call for Pension Funds to Buy Local
Yen gains after finance minister urges pension funds to invest more at home
Factbox-Japan's GPIF holds about $931 billion in foreign assets
Japan weighs dedicated agency to support Self-Defense Forces retirees

marketTone.ko: 일본 금융시장은 엔화 강세 유도 및 국내 투자 활성화를 위한 정부의 움직임에 주목하고 있습니다. 재무장관은 연기금, 특히 막대한 규모의 공적연금(GPIF)에 국내 자산 투자를 늘릴 것을 촉구했습니다. 이는 아베 시대의 자본 유출 흐름을 되돌리고 '병든 엔화'를 부양하려는 정책 전환으로 해석되며, 이미 엔화 강세에 기여하고 있습니다. GPIF가 약 9,310억 달러에 달하는 해외 자산을 보유하고 있다는 점은 이러한 정책 변화의 잠재적 파급력을 시사합니다.
marketTone.en: Japanese financial markets are reacting to government efforts aimed at strengthening the yen and promoting domestic investment. The finance minister has urged pension funds, including the massive Government Pension Investment Fund (GPIF), to increase their allocation to local assets. This policy pivot seeks to reverse the trend of capital outflow and has already contributed to gains in the yen. The move is seen as an attempt to boost the 'sickly yen' by encouraging funds to shift away from their substantial foreign asset holdings.
